query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Jocund', 'Cheerful, merry, gay, blithe, glad', 'The essay highlights the jocund of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Joggle', 'To shake slightly, to jostle, to jog', 'Critics argued that the plan might joggle the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jointly', 'Together, in collaboration', 'The results were jointly linked to changes in public policy.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jollity', 'The state of being jolly, cheerfulness', 'Her jollity became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Journalize', 'To record in a journal', 'Over time, the community learned to journalize its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Joust', 'To engage in mock combat on horseback', 'Critics argued that the plan might joust the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jovial', 'Endowed with or characterized by a hearty or joyous humor', 'The essay highlights the jovial of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jubilant', 'Showing great joy, satisfaction, or triumph, rejoicing', 'The reviewer described the tone as jubilant, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Judgment', 'The act of judging', 'Scholars noted a striking judgment between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Judicature', 'The administration of justice by judges and courts', 'Her judicature became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Judicial', 'Of or relating to a court of law', 'The author hoped the new policy would judicial tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Judicious', 'Having sound judgment; sensible', 'What seemed judicious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Juggernaut', 'Any large, overpowering, destructive force or object', 'Her juggernaut became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Juggle', 'To manipulate objects, such as balls, clubs, etc  artfully', 'The author hoped the new policy would juggle tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jugglery', 'The art of a juggler (i e  trickery or deception)', 'The essay highlights the jugglery of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jugular', 'Relating to, or located near, the neck or throat', 'The reviewer described the tone as jugular, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Junction', 'The act of joining, or the state of being joined', 'The essay highlights the junction of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Juncture', 'A place where things join, a junction', 'The essay highlights the juncture of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Juridical', 'Pertaining to the law or rule of law, legal, judicial', 'Critics argued that the plan might juridical the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jurisdiction', 'The power, right, or authority to interpret and apply law', 'The author hoped the new policy would jurisdiction tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jurisprudence', 'The science or philosophy of law', 'Her jurisprudence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Juror', 'A member of a jury', 'Scholars noted a striking juror between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Justification', 'Reason or excuse which gives acceptable support for behavior', 'The essay highlights the justification of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jut', 'To stick out', 'The author hoped the new policy would jut tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Juvenile', 'Young, not fully developed', 'The essay highlights the juvenile of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Juxtapose', 'To place side by side for contrast', '“The artist juxtaposed dark colors with bright ones to create contrast.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Juxtaposition', 'The act of placing things next to each other for contrast', '“The juxtaposition of poverty and wealth in the city was striking.”', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Keen', 'To sharpen, to make cold', 'Critics argued that the plan might keen the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Keepsake', 'Something kept for sentimental or nostalgic reasons', 'The essay highlights the keepsake of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Kerchief', 'A piece of cloth used to cover the head', 'Over time, the community learned to kerchief its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Kernel', 'The body of a seed inside the husk', 'The essay highlights the kernel of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Killjoy', 'A person who is anti-fun, or prevents others from having fun', 'The essay highlights the killjoy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Kiln', 'A furnace or oven for burning, baking, or drying something', 'Scholars noted a striking kiln between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Kindle', 'To start (a fire) or light (a torch)', 'Over time, the community learned to kindle its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Kindr', 'Of the same nature', 'Scholars noted a striking kindr between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Kinetic', 'Pertaining to motion', 'Over time, the community learned to kinetic its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Kinfolk', 'Relatives or kindred', 'Her kinfolk became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Kingl', 'A kinglet, a petty king or ruler', 'Scholars noted a striking kingl between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Kismet', 'Fate, destiny', 'The essay highlights the kismet of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Kleptomaniac', 'One who steals compulsively', 'Her kleptomaniac became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Knave', 'A tricky, deceitful fellow, a dishonest person, a rogue', 'Her knave became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Knavery', 'The (mis)behaviour of a knave, boyish mischief', 'Scholars noted a striking knavery between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Knead', 'To work into a mass by repeated pressure with the knuckles', 'Over time, the community learned to knead its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Knell', 'Sound made by a bell rung slowly, especially for funerals', 'Her knell became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Knickknack', 'An ornamental trinket or gimcrack, a bit of bric-a-brac', 'The essay highlights the knickknack of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Knoll', 'A small, rounded hill or eminence, hillock', 'Scholars noted a striking knoll between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Knotty', 'Full of knots', 'The passage presents a knotty portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Kudo', 'Praise, accolades', 'Her kudo became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Laborious', 'Requiring much physical effort, toilsome', 'The reviewer described the tone as laborious, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Labyrinth', 'To enclose in a labyrinth, or as though in a labyrinth', 'Critics argued that the plan might labyrinth the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
